originalAlarm.description: 2nd function call argument is an uninitialized value

sanitizedDescription: 2nd function call argument is an uninitialized value
model:
(define-fun APR_HAS_SHARED_MEMORY () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __need___FILE () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __malloc_and_calloc_defined () Bool
  false)
reasoning: d05e574ec26.c L17: `printf("%s\n", buf)` where buf may be null/uninitialized. At L16: `char* buf = apr_rmm_addr_get((char*)calloc(rmm_addr, sizeof(char)))` — rmm_addr=99999999999999999 is extremely large, causing calloc to almost certainly return NULL. apr_rmm_addr_get(NULL) then dereferences the null pointer. buf could be NULL/undefined when passed to printf. New bug (no ERROR). Real condition: APR_HAS_SHARED_MEMORY=T. PC: APR_HAS_SHARED_MEMORY=T AND !__need___FILE AND !_STDLIB_H AND !__malloc_and_calloc_defined. The library macros (!_STDLIB_H, !__malloc_and_calloc_defined) are restrictive, meaning the PC only fires in non-standard stdlib.h states. Over-constrained — the bug also exists when stdlib.h is fully included (normal compilation state).
